lede: A look at March 26 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jesús Fortea and Emma Laine, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Francis Scott Key Bridge (Baltimore)
events.desc: The Francis Scott Key Bridge collapses following a collision between the MV Dali container ship and one of the bridge's support pillars, killing six people.

events.title: Singapore Airlines Flight 117
events.desc: Singapore Airlines Flight 117 is hijacked by four Pakistani terrorists and diverted to Changi Airport.

events.title: Biological Weapons Convention
events.desc: The Biological Weapons Convention comes into force.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: First Battle of Gaza: British troops are halted after 17,000 Turks block their advance.

events.title: First Balkan War
events.desc: First Balkan War: Bulgarian forces capture Adrianople.

events.title: Brunner Mine disaster
events.desc: An explosion at the Brunner Mine near Greymouth, New Zealand, kills 65 coal miners in the country's worst industrial accident.

events.title: Métis
events.desc: The Métis people of the District of Saskatchewan under Louis Riel begin the North-West Rebellion against Canada.

events.title: Gerrymandering
events.desc: A political cartoon in the Boston-Gazette coins the term "gerrymander" to describe oddly shaped electoral districts designed to help incumbents win reelection.

events.title: William Caxton
events.desc: William Caxton prints his translation of Aesop's Fables.

events.title: Saladin
events.desc: Saladin becomes the emir of Egypt.

births.name: Kate Richards O'Hare
births.line: American Socialist Party activist and editor (died 1948)

deaths.text: Noelia Castillo euthanasia case, Spanish woman who died after receiving euthanasia
Alice Herz, German peace activist who self-immolated in protest of U.S. imperialism (born 1882) 1
Cecil Rhodes, English-South African colonialist, businessman and politician, 6th Prime Minister of the Cape Colony

observances.text: Christian feast days: Harriet Monsell (Church of England)
